did.head: DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY
did.physdesc: Correspondence, organizational, legal and financil papers.
did.repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell
University Library
did.abstract:
Letters and other material relating to
patronage and machine politics of the Democratic Party in Seneca County.

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p:
Diedrich Willers Sr. was born in Germany and came to the United States
after having served with the Hanoverian forces at the Battle of Waterloo.
Diedrich Willers Jr. (1833-1908) was secretary of New York State (1878), and a
Democratic assemblyman from Seneca County.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p:
Letters and other material relating to patronage and machine politics
of the Democratic Party in Seneca County; other topics include draft
exemptions, laws relating to the hiring of substitutes in the Civil War,
pardons, Poor House investigations, canals and canal appointments, letters
concerning Governor Tilden's policies, Horatio Seymour's stand on the subject
of bounties, and actions taken by the Romulus Convention (1875). Also, papers
relating to the German Reformed Church at Bearytown, Seneca County, New
York.

unittitle.extref: Link to a PDF finding aid.

scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p:
Willers family papers, #652. Division of Rare and Manuscript
Collections, Cornell University Library.
